page: Microsoft Azure D8nls_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnlv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 8573C processor cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and INT8 operations.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, requiring external premium storage options for persistent disk needs. The server is optimized for network-heavy workloads, leveraging its dedicated compute resources and high-speed network interfaces to maintain throughput.

Performance benchmarks place the server in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core score of 7031.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 12481.2. Memory bandwidth reaches 545.1 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 63.58 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. In language model inference tests, it demonstrates strong text generation performance with a small 135M model at 264.1 tokens/sec, though text generation drops to a weak 8.19 tokens/sec when scaling to a medium 7B model.

Given its low memory-to-core ratio and absence of bundled local storage, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, network-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for network-optimized tasks, static web serving (benchmarked at 13.36 GB/sec), Redis key-value stores (achieving 9.63 million SET operations per second), and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D8nls_v6 is an x86_64 network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnlv6 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) running at 4.2 GHz, backed by a 260 MB L3 cache and 16.0 GB of RAM. This low-memory configuration (2.0 GB per core) does not include local storage but supports premium external storage. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 7031.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 12481.2. It shows strong performance in small-model text generation (264.1 tokens/sec) and handles 9.63 million Redis SET operations per second. The server is designed for network-bound workloads, static web serving, and lightweight compute tasks where high memory density is not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8nls_v6 is an x86_64 network-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for compute-heavy and network-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8nls_v6 is an 8-vCPU network-optimized server with 16.0 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz.
tagline: A network-optimized, low-memory compute instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for efficient throughput.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
16.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio.
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512.
No local storage included; premium external storage capable.
Optimized for network-heavy workloads, static web serving, and Redis databases.
